note: Recorded reference run. Verdicts produced by the Critic agent attacking each Modeller axiom against its verbatim source span. Reproduce live with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set: python3 scripts/multi_agent_pipeline.py.

results.name: HighRiskAISystem
results.axiom: euai:HighRiskAISystem owl:equivalentClass [ AISystem and (operatesInDomain some AnnexIIIDomain) ]
results.flaw: The equivalentClass axiom claims high-risk == Annex III systems exactly, but Article 6(1) makes a second class of systems high-risk: safety components of products covered by the Annex I harmonisation legislation. The Annex III route alone does not capture all high-risk systems.
results.recommended_fix: Either document the Article 6(1) product-safety route as deliberately out of scope for the PoC, or model it as a second branch and weaken the equivalence.

results.name: SocialScoring
results.flaw: Minor: the prohibition is qualified by sub-conditions (i)/(ii) (detrimental treatment in unrelated contexts, or unjustified/disproportionate treatment), which the flat class abstracts away. Acceptable for a proof of concept.
results.recommended_fix: Optionally annotate that the qualifying conditions are abstracted.

results.name: RealTimeRemoteBiometricID
results.flaw: Over-claim. The span itself says the use is prohibited 'unless and in so far as such use is strictly necessary' for narrowly listed law-enforcement objectives (e.g. targeted search for victims, threat to life, locating a suspect of a serious offence), subject to authorisation. Asserting an unconditional prohibition contradicts the text.
results.recommended_fix: Model as a conditionally-prohibited practice, or annotate the exceptions as explicitly out of scope.

results.name: MarketSurveillanceAuthority
results.flaw: None. A market surveillance authority enforces the Act and is not a provider/importer/distributor, so aligning it to airo:Stakeholder rather than airo:AIOperator is correct.

results.name: MinimalRisk
results.flaw: Ungrounded in the cited provision. The span concerns voluntary codes of conduct (Article 95) and does not name a 'minimal risk' tier; the term is recital-level only. The RiskLevel individual is not anchored in an article.
results.recommended_fix: Keep but annotate as a recital-derived category, or drop for a stricter article-only model.

results.name: Provider
results.flaw: None. The span defines 'provider' as a party that develops an AI system or GPAI model and places it on the market; subclassing airo:AIProvider is faithful.

results.name: ob_RiskManagement
results.axiom: euai:ob_RiskManagement a euai:RiskManagementObligation ; euai:imposedOn euai:Provider ; euai:appliesToSystem euai:HighRiskAISystem
results.flaw: None material. Article 9 requires a risk management system for high-risk systems; Article 16(a) makes providers responsible for compliance, so imposedOn Provider is correct.

results.name: ob_DeepfakeDisclosure
results.axiom: euai:ob_DeepfakeDisclosure a euai:TransparencyObligation ; euai:imposedOn euai:Deployer ; euai:appliesToSystem euai:LimitedRiskAISystem
results.flaw: Over-claim. Article 50(4) attaches exceptions: the obligation is relaxed for law-enforcement use, and for evidently artistic, creative, satirical or fictional works the disclosure is limited so as not to hamper enjoyment. Modelling it as an unconditional obligation omits these.
results.recommended_fix: Annotate the exceptions, or mark the obligation as conditional.

results.name: AISystem
results.flaw: None. The span reproduces the Article 3(1) definition almost verbatim of airo:AISystem; the equivalence is faithful.

results.name: EmploymentAndWorkerManagement
results.flaw: None. The span lists 'Employment, workers management and access to self-employment' as an Annex III area.
